backlog.title: QA Bug Fix Remediation
backlog.description: Remediate the findings from the end-to-end QA pass against the skpp PRD. One Major issue (discovery source not surfaced in --path) and six Minor issues (unicode table alignment, .gitignore deviation, search field inconsistency, combined-flag parsing, listing-mode exclusivity, check rule documentation). All core contracts (path resolution, exit codes, stdout discipline, atomicity, discovery priority) are confirmed working; this phase addresses the surfaced gaps only.

milestones.title: Major Fix — Discovery Source Reporting
milestones.description: Resolve Issue 1 (Major): `skpp --path` must report which of the three §8 discovery rules (env var, sibling-of-binary, walk-up) produced the result. The Source enum, its String() method, and its unit tests already exist in skillsdir.go but the value is discarded to `_` in main.go's --path branch. This milestone wires the existing data into the --path output path.

tasks.title: Surface discovery rule in --path output
tasks.description: Bind the Source return value from skillsdir.Find() in the --path branch of main.go and print the human-readable source label to stderr. stdout must remain byte-exact (dir + newline) to preserve the §13 acceptance gate. Update existing tests that assert empty stderr on --path success.

subtasks.title: Wire Source into --path stderr reporting and update tests
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_fcde63e5bb60/bugfix/001_7fdd8ce7410b/architecture/issue_analysis.md §Issue 1 and decisions.md §D1. The skillsdir.Source enum (skillsdir.go:25-34) already has SourceEnv/SourceSibling/SourceWalkUp with String() labels ("SKPP_SKILLS_DIR" / "sibling of binary" / "ancestor of cwd" / "unknown"), all unit-tested (skillsdir_test.go:30-52 TestSourceString). Find() (skillsdir.go:232) returns (dir string, src Source, err error). main.go --path branch (main.go:268-281) currently binds src to `_`: `dir, _, err := skillsdir.Find()`.
2. INPUT: The existing skillsdir.Source type and its String() method. No new types, no skillsdir changes.
3. LOGIC: In main.go's `c.path` branch (run() function), change `dir, _, err` to `dir, src, err`. After the existing `fmt.Fprintln(stdout, dir)` line (which MUST stay byte-exact — it is locked by the §13 gate `test "$(./skpp --path)" = "$PWD/skills"`), add: `fmt.Fprintf(stderr, "(found via %s)\n", src)`. The Source.String() method provides the label. Do NOT print the source to stdout under any circumstances. PRD §8: "skpp --path reports which rule won."
4. OUTPUT: `--path` now writes `<dir>\n` to stdout AND `(found via <rule>)\n` to stderr on success. On failure (ErrNotFound), behavior is unchanged (nothing on stdout, error to stderr, exit 1). The §13 acceptance gate still passes because $() captures stdout only.
5. TESTS (implied TDD): UPDATE main_test.go TestRunPathSuccess (line ~169) which currently asserts `errOut.Len() != 0` → it must now expect `(found via SKPP_SKILLS_DIR)\n` on stderr when SKPP_SKILLS_DIR is set via t.Setenv. UPDATE TestRunPathShortFlag (line ~187) similarly. ADD a test asserting the stderr label matches SourceEnv when env is set. The sibling/walk-up Source values are hard to exercise through run() (they depend on binary path / cwd) so testing the env case via t.Setenv is sufficient.
6. DOCS: [Mode A] none — the --path help text in usageText (main.go const usageText, line ~44) stays the same (it still says "Print the resolved skills directory"); the stderr source label is a diagnostic addition with no new flag surface. README overview update is deferred to the final Mode B doc-sync task P1.M5.T3.

milestones.title: Search Field Expansion
milestones.description: Resolve Issue 4 (Minor): `--search` must also match metadata.aliases and metadata.category, resolving the §6.1 vs §10 spec tension in favor of §10 (which explicitly states keywords/category/aliases exist to enrich --search). The Skill struct already carries Aliases and Category fields populated by BuildSkill; only the search.matches() function omits them.

tasks.title: Extend --search to match aliases and category
tasks.description: Add Aliases (each element individually, like Keywords) and Category substring matching to search.matches(). Invert the existing test that asserts they do NOT match.

subtasks.title: Add alias/category matching to search.matches() and invert test
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/issue_analysis.md §Issue 4 and decisions.md §D4. search.go:59 matches() currently scans exactly four fields: s.RelTag, s.Name, s.Description, and each element of s.Keywords (search.go:59-77). The Skill struct (skill.go:42-52) carries Aliases []string (populated from metadata.aliases by BuildSkill at skill.go:105-121) and Category string (from metadata.category). Both are populated and available at search time. The existing test TestSearchDoesNotMatchCategoryOrAliases (search_test.go:126) asserts the OPPOSITE of the desired behavior and must be inverted.
2. INPUT: The existing discover.Skill struct with Aliases []string and Category string fields. No struct changes.
3. LOGIC: In search.go matches() (line 59), after the Keywords `for` loop (the last block before `return false`), add two checks mirroring the Keywords pattern: (a) `for _, a := range s.Aliases { if strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(a), q) { return true } }` — Aliases matched INDIVIDUALLY (same boundary-safety rationale as Keywords). (b) `if strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(s.Category), q) { return true }`. Update the matches() doc comment (search.go:49-53) which currently says it "deliberately does NOT include Category or Aliases" — rewrite to state they ARE now included per PRD §10.
4. OUTPUT: `skpp --search <alias>` and `skpp --search <category-substring>` now return matching skills. Resolution by alias (skpp <alias>) was already working (resolve.go §7.2 step 4); this makes search consistent.
5. TESTS (implied TDD): REPLACE search_test.go TestSearchDoesNotMatchCategoryOrAliases (line 126) with TestSearchMatchesCategoryAndAliases that asserts Search("secret-alias", skillWithAliases) returns the skill and Search("secret-cat", skillWithCategory) returns the skill. Keep the keyword-boundary test (TestSearchKeywordSubstringNotJoinBoundary) unchanged — aliases also matched individually.
6. DOCS: [Mode A] Update the --search description in main.go usageText const (line ~44, currently "Substring search over tag / name / description / keywords") to read "Substring search over tag / name / description / keywords / aliases / category". This rides WITH the work — do not create a separate docs subtask.

milestones.title: Unicode Table Alignment
milestones.description: Resolve Issue 2 (Minor): the --list/--search table misaligns when tags or descriptions contain multi-byte UTF-8 characters. The root cause is byte-length (len()) used for column width in padRight, the width computation, and wrapWords. The fix uses the stdlib unicode/utf8 rune count — no new dependency, respecting the §4/§7.3 single-dependency policy.

tasks.title: Fix unicode display width in ui table rendering
tasks.description: Replace byte-length width calculations with a rune-count-based displayWidth helper across padRight, column-width computation, and wrapWords. Fix the false ASCII assumption comment.

subtasks.title: Implement rune-count displayWidth and fix padRight, wrapWords, column math, comment
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/issue_analysis.md §Issue 2 and decisions.md §D2. ui.go:132 padRight uses `len(s)` (byte length) — multi-byte UTF-8 runes (é=2 bytes, —=3 bytes) are under-counted, shifting columns left. Three sites use byte length: (a) padRight (ui.go:133 `if len(s) >= n` and ui.go:136 `n-len(s)`), (b) column-width computation (ui.go:79-82 `len(s.RelTag)` and `len(name)`), (c) wrapWords (ui.go:156 `len(cur)+1+len(word)`). The false ASCII comment is at ui.go:128-131. Key insight: RelTag and Name ARE charset-constrained (check.go validName regex enforces a-z0-9-), so byte==display for those today; Description is free-form prose with NO charset restriction (only length-checked) and is the realistic misalignment vector.
2. INPUT: No new types. Import `unicode/utf8` (Go stdlib, zero new dependencies — respects PRD §4/§7.3).
3. LOGIC: Add a package-level helper `func displayWidth(s string) int { return utf8.RuneCountInString(s) }`. Replace every `len(s)` used for DISPLAY width with `displayWidth(s)` at the three sites: (a) padRight body — `if displayWidth(s) >= n { return s }` and `return s + strings.Repeat(" ", n-displayWidth(s))`; (b) column-width loop — `if displayWidth(s.RelTag) > tagW` and `if displayWidth(name) > nameW`; (c) wrapWords — `case displayWidth(cur)+1+displayWidth(word) <= width`. Fix the comment at ui.go:128-131 to state that width is rune-count-based (handles common multi-byte runes like é, —, smart quotes) and note the known limitation that wide CJK runes (display width 2) are not fully handled (would need a width table, deliberately avoided per §4/§7.3 dependency policy). Do NOT change the paint-before-color invariant (ui.go:88-92) — color escape bytes must stay out of width math.
4. OUTPUT: Tables align correctly for multi-byte tags and descriptions. ASCII-only output is unchanged (rune count == byte count for ASCII).
5. TESTS (implied TDD): ADD ui_test.go test using a skill with a multi-byte tag (e.g. "café") and/or a multi-byte description (e.g. containing "—" or "é") and assert column alignment via the existing colOf() helper — every row's DESCRIPTION column must start at the same byte offset. TestPrintListColumnsAlignedAcrossRows (ui_test.go:153, ASCII-only) must still pass unchanged.
6. DOCS: [Mode A] none — internal formatting fix, no user-facing/config/API surface change.

milestones.title: CLI Ergonomics Polish
milestones.description: Resolve Issues 5 and 6 (both Minor UX): combined short flags and --flag=value syntax are rejected as unknown flags, and combining two listing modes silently picks one by dispatch order. Both touch main.go but different functions (parseArgs vs exclusivityError/run dispatch).

tasks.title: Support combined short flags and --flag=value syntax
tasks.description: Normalize argv tokens in parseArgs before the exact-match switch so that --flag=value and -abc (combined short bool flags) are accepted. The short flag set is v h p l a f s; only -s/--search takes a value.

subtasks.title: Normalize tokens in parseArgs before the switch
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/issue_analysis.md §Issue 5 and decisions.md §D5. main.go:145 parseArgs uses `switch a` (main.go:152) that matches exact whole tokens only. `-vh` does not match `case "-v"`; `--version=x` does not match `case "--version"` → both fall to the default branch → captured as unknownFlag → exit 2 (main.go:194-198). The short flag set with single-dash forms is: v (--version), h (--help), p (--path), l (--list), a (--all), f (--file), s (--search). --relative and --no-color are LONG-ONLY (no short alias, cannot appear in a bundle). Only --search/-s is value-taking.
2. INPUT: The existing parseArgs function and config struct. No new fields.
3. LOGIC: At the TOP of the parseArgs for-loop body (before `switch a`), normalize the token `a` into a canonical form: (a) LONG FLAGS with `=`: if `a` starts with `--` and contains `=`, split into flag and value parts. Map the flag part to its case (e.g. `--search` → set searchMode=true and searchQ=value; `--version` → set version=true, ignore the value for bool flags). (b) SHORT BUNDLES: if `a` starts with single `-` (not `--`), has length > 2, and every char is a known short bool flag (v/h/p/l/a/f), expand: set each corresponding config field to true. If the bundle contains `s` (the value-taking short flag), `s` must be LAST in the bundle and the remainder after `s` (or the next arg) is the search query (e.g. `-sfoo` → search "foo", `-ls foo` → list=true AND search "foo"). An unknown char in a bundle → unknownFlag. (c) The `check` positional token and bare tags (no leading `-`) pass through unchanged to the existing default branch.
4. OUTPUT: `-vh`, `-pl`, `--version=x`, `--search=foo`, `-sfoo` all work. Existing exact-token forms (`--version`, `-v`, `--search foo`) still work unchanged. Unknown flags still exit 2.
5. TESTS (implied TDD): ADD main_test.go tests: parseArgs(["-vh"]) → version=true, help=true; parseArgs(["--path=/x"]) → path=true (value ignored for bool); parseArgs(["--search=foo"]) → searchMode=true, searchQ="foo"; parseArgs(["-sfoo"]) → searchMode=true, searchQ="foo"; parseArgs(["-af"]) → all=true, file=true. Verify unknown char in bundle (e.g. `-vz`) sets unknownFlag. Existing parseArgs tests (TestParseArgs*) must still pass.
6. DOCS: [Mode A] none — the flags themselves do not change (no new flag surface); only how they are parsed. The usageText already lists the short forms.

tasks.title: Reject conflicting listing modes
tasks.description: Extend exclusivityError to reject any combination of two or more listing modes (--path, --list, --search, --all), and fix the stale dispatch-order comment in main.go run().

subtasks.title: Extend exclusivityError for mode+mode combos and fix stale dispatch comment
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/issue_analysis.md §Issue 6 and decisions.md §D6. exclusivityError (main.go:484-497) currently checks three families: tags+(list/search/all), check+tags, check+(list/search/all). It does NOT reject mode+mode combos like --list --search. The dispatch order in run() is path > list > search > check > all > tags (confirmed by the top-level if-chain: path=main.go:268, list=283, search=321, check=361, all=402, tags=433). The stale comment at main.go:266-267 ("// 5) Normal mode dispatch (order unchanged): check → path → list → search → all → tags") is WRONG — check is step 8 (after path/list/search), not first.
2. INPUT: The existing exclusivityError function and config struct.
3. LOGIC: In exclusivityError (main.go:484), add a 4th family BEFORE the existing checks: count how many of {c.path, c.list, c.searchMode, c.all} are true; if >= 2, return (true, "skpp: listing modes --path/--list/--search/--all are mutually exclusive"). Also fix the stale comment at main.go:266-267 to reflect the ACTUAL dispatch order: path → list → search → check → all → tags. Note: check is standalone-safe because exclusivityError already rejects check+mode and check+tags, so check's late position in dispatch is harmless.
4. OUTPUT: `--list --search foo`, `--all --list`, `--path --list` now exit 2 with a clear message. Existing tags+mode, check+tags, check+mode exclusivity unchanged.
5. TESTS (implied TDD): ADD main_test.go tests: run(["--list", "--search", "foo"]) → exit 2, stderr contains "mutually exclusive"; run(["--all", "--list"]) → exit 2; run(["--path", "--list"]) → exit 2. Existing exclusivity tests (TestRunExclusivity* or similar) must still pass. NOTE: this subtask and P1.M4.T1.S1 both modify main.go but touch different functions (exclusivityError/run vs parseArgs); if P1.M4.T1.S1 has already landed, line numbers may have shifted but the functions are non-overlapping.
6. DOCS: [Mode A] none — no new flag surface; the exclusivity error message is runtime output, not documented config.

milestones.title: Spec Alignment & Documentation
milestones.description: Resolve Issues 3 and 7 (both Minor), plus the changeset-level documentation sweep (Mode B). Issue 3 aligns .gitignore with PRD §16. Issue 7 confirms the deliberate omission of the check 'no SKILL.md' rule needs no code change. The final task syncs README.md and overview docs to reflect the full changeset.

tasks.title: Align .gitignore with PRD §16 specification
tasks.description: Trim the shipped .gitignore to the exact 5-entry set specified in PRD §16, removing the undocumented extras (/build, .env, .env.*, .pi-subagents/).

subtasks.title: Trim .gitignore to the §16 spec set
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/issue_analysis.md §Issue 3 and decisions.md §D3. The current .gitignore has 9 entries in 4 sections. PRD §16 specifies EXACTLY these 5 entries (no comments, no sections): `/skpp`, `/dist`, `*.test`, `*.out`, `.DS_Store`. The extras to remove are: `/build`, `.env`, `.env.*`, `.pi-subagents/`. PRD.md cannot be modified (read-only, human-owned), so the implementation must conform to the spec.
2. INPUT: The PRD §16 specification (the canonical 5-entry list).
3. LOGIC: Overwrite .gitignore with exactly the 5 entries from §16, preserving the order and format from the PRD. Remove all section comments and extra entries.
4. OUTPUT: `cat .gitignore` matches §16 exactly. `git status` may show .pi-subagents/ artifacts as untracked after this change — that is expected and correct (the spec does not ignore them).
5. TESTS: Verify by diffing .gitignore against the §16 block in PRD.md. No Go tests.
6. DOCS: [Mode A] none — .gitignore is itself the spec artifact.

tasks.title: Confirm check 'no SKILL.md' rule documentation
tasks.description: Issue 7 is effectively moot: the §9 'skill dir has no SKILL.md' rule cannot fire because discover.Index only emits directories that contain SKILL.md. The check.go code already documents this as deliberate. Verify the comment is accurate and complete; no code change is required or desired (a heuristic would false-positive on legitimate grouping directories).

subtasks.title: Verify check.go omission rationale is accurately documented
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/issue_analysis.md §Issue 7 and decisions.md §D7. check.go:123-129 already documents WHY the §9 'skill dir has no SKILL.md' rule is not implemented: discover.Index (index.go:46) defines a skill as 'any directory that directly contains SKILL.md' — a grouping dir without SKILL.md is simply never indexed, never checked. The §9 rule is REFRAMED in check.go:150 as 'invalid SKILL.md frontmatter' (an unreadable/malformed file is the actionable version). A heuristic (e.g. flagging dirs with scripts/ but no SKILL.md) would false-positive on legitimate grouping dirs.
2. INPUT: The existing check.go doc comment (lines 123-129) and the check.go:150-151 reframed ERROR.
3. LOGIC: Read check.go:123-129 and verify the comment accurately explains: (a) why the rule cannot fire (Index only emits dirs with SKILL.md), (b) why a heuristic is rejected (false-positives on grouping dirs), (c) that the rule is reframed as 'invalid SKILL.md frontmatter' (check.go:150). If the comment is already complete and accurate, NO code change is needed — this subtask is a verification gate that confirms the decision is documented. If the comment is unclear or incomplete, refine it ONLY to improve clarity (do not add code).
4. OUTPUT: The check.go rationale comment is confirmed accurate (or clarified). No behavioral change. The §13 acceptance gate (`skpp check` reports example as OK) must still pass.
5. TESTS: Run `go test ./internal/check/` and verify all pass. Run `./skpp check` and verify the example skill reports OK. No new tests needed.
6. DOCS: [Mode A] The check.go comment IS the documentation for this decision. If clarified, the clarification rides with this subtask.

tasks.title: Sync changeset-level documentation
tasks.description: Mode B final documentation sweep: update README.md and any overview docs to reflect the full changeset. This task depends on ALL implementing subtasks so it runs last and captures the coherent delta.

subtasks.title: Update README.md to reflect --path source reporting and search field expansion
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: This is the Mode B changeset-level documentation sweep (per SOW §5). README.md mirrors the mcpeepants README structure (PRD §15 outline). It documents --path, --search, and the discovery rules. The changeset introduced: (a) --path now reports the discovery source rule on stderr (P1.M1.T1.S1), (b) --search now matches aliases and category in addition to tag/name/description/keywords (P1.M2.T1.S1), (c) tables now handle unicode display width (P1.M3.T1.S1), (d) combined short flags and --flag=value are accepted (P1.M4.T1.S1), (e) conflicting listing modes now error (P1.M4.T2.S1).
2. INPUT: The completed code changes from all implementing subtasks. Read README.md and identify every section that references --path, --search fields, flag syntax, or listing-mode behavior.
3. LOGIC: Update README.md sections: (a) Where --path is documented, note that it also prints the discovery source (env var / sibling of binary / ancestor of cwd) to stderr for diagnostics. (b) Where --search is documented, update the searchable-fields list to include aliases and category. (c) If README shows flag syntax examples, optionally mention combined shorts and --flag=value are supported. (d) If README documents mode exclusivity, note listing modes are mutually exclusive. Do NOT modify PRD.md (read-only). Keep the README's existing tone and structure (PRD §15 outline).
4. OUTPUT: README.md accurately reflects the shipped behavior. No stale claims about --path, --search fields, or flag parsing remain.
5. TESTS: Manual review — verify README claims match the actual binary behavior by spot-checking `./skpp --path`, `./skpp --search`, and `./skpp --help`.
6. DOCS: This IS the documentation task (Mode B). It must run AFTER all implementing subtasks (dependencies listed above) so it captures the final coherent state.
